data.title: Analyst Reiterates Hold on AEP as Shares Trade Above $133 Price Target Despite Modest EPS Growth Outlook
data.description:
Ross Fowler, an analyst from Bank of America Securities, reiterated the Hold rating on American Electric Power. The associated price target is $133.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ross Fowler has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a modest earnings outlook and valuation that already reflects much of the upside. He expects AEP’s Q2 2026 operating EPS to rise slightly versus last year, driven primarily by growth in vertically integrated utility operations and incremental contributions from transmission and distribution businesses.

However, this earnings improvement is partially offset by rising operating costs, higher depreciation, and increased interest expense as the company executes a sizable capital plan. While he anticipates management will reaffirm its long-term growth objectives and sees realistic prospects for higher returns on equity, the current share price exceeds his $133 price objective, limiting near-term appreciation potential and supporting a Neutral (Hold) stance.

In another report released on July 22, BMO Capital also assigned a Hold rating to the stock with a $139.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Solid Fundamentals and Credit Trends; $29 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
In a report released today, Caroline Latta from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating on SLM, with a price target of $29.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Caroline Latta has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that, in her view, outweigh the headline EPS shortfall in the recent quarter. She highlights that the earnings miss was driven primarily by a temporary spike in provisions linked to changes in recovery practices, while management reaffirmed full-year EPS, loan origination, and expense guidance, signaling confidence in underlying performance.

Latta also points to SLM’s solid competitive positioning, long-term upside from the Grad PLUS opportunity, and a supportive funding structure that underpins capital return to shareholders. She expects net interest margin to improve in the second half as excess liquidity is deployed, and notes that credit trends, including delinquencies and loan-mod performance, are slightly better than previously anticipated, supporting her unchanged $29 price target.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $28.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Prime Medicine Buy Rating Reiterated as Pipeline Advances; $6 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Cory Jubinville, PhD maintained a Buy rating on Prime Medicine, Inc. today and set a price target of $6.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Cory Jubinville, PhD has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Prime Medicine’s emerging clinical pipeline and regulatory momentum. The recent FDA clearance of the IND for PM577a in Wilson disease, alongside the earlier New Zealand CTA, establishes a global Phase 1/2 program that is expected to begin in the second half of 2026 and generate initial clinical readouts in 2027, providing a clear path to value-inflecting milestones.

Jubinville also highlights that PM577a is designed to correct the most common ATP7B mutation driving Wilson disease in North America and Europe, giving the program substantial addressable-market relevance while follow-on candidates, such as an R778L-focused therapy for East Asian patients, expand the broader opportunity. Despite a relatively short cash runway and notable short interest, he maintains that the company’s focused execution, multi-asset rare disease strategy, and defined upcoming catalysts support a favorable risk/reward profile and justify reiterating a Buy rating with an unchanged $6 price target.

In another report released on July 9, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $8.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
